2603.13676.title: TheraAgent: Multi-Agent Framework with Self-Evolving Memory and Evidence-Calibrated Reasoning for PET Theranostics
2603.13676.paper_summary: PET theranostics is transforming precision oncology, yet treatment response varies substantially; many patients receiving 177Lu-PSMA radioligand therapy (RLT) for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) fail to respond, demanding reliable pre-therapy prediction. While LLM-based agents have shown remarkable potential in complex medical diagnosis, their application to PET theranostic outcome prediction remains unexplored, which faces three key challenges: (1) data and knowledge scarcity: RLT was only FDA-approved in 2022, yielding few training cases and insufficient domain knowledge in general LLMs; (2) heterogeneous information integration: robust prediction hinges on structured knowledge extraction from PET/CT, laboratory tests, and free-text clinical documentation; (3) evidence-grounded reasoning: clinical decisions must be anchored in trial evidence rather than LLM hallucinations. In this paper, we present TheraAgent, to our knowledge, the first agentic framework for PET theranostics, with three core innovations: (1) Multi-Expert Feature Extraction with Confidence-Weighted Consensus, where three specialized experts process heterogeneous inputs with uncertainty quantification; (2) Self-Evolving Agentic Memory (SEA-Mem), which learns prognostic patterns from accumulated cases, enabling case-based reasoning from limited data; (3) Evidence-Calibrated Reasoning, integrating a curated theranostics knowledge base to ground predictions in VISION/TheraP trial evidence. Evaluated on 35 real patients and 400 synthetic cases, TheraAgent achieves 75.7% overall accuracy on real patients and 87.0% on synthetic cases, outperforming MDAgents and MedAgent-Pro by over 20%. These results highlight a promising blueprint for trustworthy AI agents in PET theranostics, enabling trial-calibrated, multi-source decision support. Code will be released upon acceptance.
2603.13676.paper_summary_zh: PET 影像治療學正在改變精準腫瘤學，但治療反應差異顯著；許多接受 177Lu-PSMA 放射配體療法 (RLT) 的轉移性去勢抵抗性前列腺癌 (mCRPC) 患者未能產生反應，因此需要可靠的治療前預測。雖然基於大型語言模型 (LLM) 的代理在複雜醫療診斷中顯示出顯著潛力，但其在 PET 影像治療學結果預測中的應用仍未被探索，面臨三個主要挑戰：(1) 數據和知識稀缺：RLT 於 2022 年才獲得 FDA 批准，產生的訓練案例有限，且一般 LLM 中的領域知識不足；(2) 異質信息整合：穩健的預測依賴於從 PET/CT、實驗室檢測和自由文本臨床文件中提取結構化知識；(3) 基於證據的推理：臨床決策必須基於試驗證據，而非 LLM 的幻覺。在本文中，我們提出 TheraAgent，據我們所知，這是首個針對 PET 影像治療學的代理框架，具有三項核心創新：(1) 具有信心加權共識的多專家特徵提取，三位專家處理異質輸入並進行不確定性量化；(2) 自我演化的代理記憶 (SEA-Mem)，從累積案例中學習預後模式，使得從有限數據中進行基於案例的推理成為可能；(3) 證據校準推理，整合精心策劃的影像治療學知識庫，以將預測基於 VISION/TheraP 試驗證據。經過對 35 名真實患者和 400 個合成案例的評估，TheraAgent 在真實患者上的整體準確率達到 75.7%，在合成案例上達到 87.0%，超過 MDAgents 和 MedAgent-Pro 超過 20%。這些結果突顯了在 PET 影像治療學中值得信賴的 AI 代理的前景藍圖，實現了試驗校準的多來源決策支持。代碼將在接受後釋出。
2603.13676.author: Zhihao Chen et.al.
2603.13676.authors: Zhihao Chen, Jiahui Wang, Yizhou Chen, Xiaozhong Ji, Xiaobin Hu, Jimin Hong, Wolfram Andreas Bosbach, Axel Rominger, Ali Afshar-Oromieh, Hongming Shan, Kuangyu Shi
